TEXT.id: PaiwanCh2_050_THE PENIS WHICH BECAME A MAN
TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: naizua tia Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu katua sedjeɫ a qalitsi.
S.PHON: naizua tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katua səɉəʎ a qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: naizua tia Kuljeljuljelju katua sedjelj a qalici.
S.PHON: naizua tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katua səɉəʎ a qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: There were Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu and just a penis.

S.FORM: pinatsayan anga nua kina katua kama.
S.PHON: pinaʦajan aŋa nua kina katua kama.
S.FORM: pinacayan anga nua kina katua kama.
S.PHON: pinaʦajan aŋa nua kina katua kama.
S.TRANSL: They had been left orphans by their parents.

S.FORM: sa vaik a kisudju a sema Pakuvadavaday ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu, sa katsu-i azua a kisudju azua qalitsi a pinituvung.
S.PHON: sa vaik a kisuɉu a səma pakuvadavadaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu, sa kaʦu-i azua a kisuɉu azua qaɭiʦi a pinituvuŋ.
S.FORM: sa vaik a kisudju a sema Pakuvadavaday ti Kuljeljuljelju, sa kacu-i azua a kisudju azua qalici a pinituvung.
S.PHON: sa vaik a kisuɉu a səma pakuvadavadaj ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu, sa kaʦu-i azua a kisuɉu azua qaɭiʦi a pinituvuŋ.
S.TRANSL: Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu went courting to Pakuvadavaday, and took along the penis in a container to go courting.

S.FORM: saka nu semenay, a kiseɫang azua qalitsi.
S.PHON: saka nu səmənaj, a kisəʎaŋ azua qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: saka nu semenay, a kiseljang azua qalici.
S.PHON: saka nu səmənaj, a kisəʎaŋ azua qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: When they sang, the penis sang along too.

S.FORM: saka maɫia, sa vaik a sema tjatjan azua maresudjusudju a tia Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu katia Muakakai katia Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka maʎia, sa vaik a səma cacan azua marəsuɉusuɉu a tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia muakakai katia cukucuku.
S.FORM: saka maljia, sa vaik a sema tjatjan azua maresudjusudju a tia Kuljeljuljelju katia Muakakai katia Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka maʎia, sa vaik a səma ʦaʦan azua marəsuɉusuɉu a tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia muakakai katia ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Next day the boy- and girl-friends went to the spring- Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu and Muakakai and Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: ti Muakakai a sangasangasan, saka ti Tjukutjuku, saka ti Qalitsi, saka ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu a viliviliɫan.
S.PHON: ti muakakai a saŋasaŋasan, saka ti cukucuku, saka ti qaɭiʦi, saka ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a viɭiviɭiʎan.
S.FORM: ti Muakakai a sangasangasan, saka ti Tjukutjuku, saka ti Qalici, saka ti Kuljeljuljelju a vilivililjan.
S.PHON: ti muakakai a saŋasaŋasan, saka ti ʦukuʦuku, saka ti qaɭiʦi, saka ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a viɭiviɭiʎan.
S.TRANSL: Muakakai was first, then Tjukutjuku, then Penis, and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu last.

S.FORM: mangtjez i tjatjan; temalaw a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: maŋcəz i cacan; təmaɭaw a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: mangtjez i tjatjan; temalaw a mapuljat.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz i ʦaʦan; təmaɭaw a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: They got to the spring, and all dipped out water.

S.FORM: ka kiramata, patsual azua qalitsi, tjaluktjuk a pasa puzaɫuman.
S.PHON: ka kiramata, paʦuaɭ azua qaɭiʦi, caɭukcuk a pasa puzaʎuman.
S.FORM: ka kiramata, pacual azua qalici, tjaluktjuk a pasa puzaljuman.
S.PHON: ka kiramata, paʦuaɭ azua qaɭiʦi, ʦaɭukʦuk a pasa puzaʎuman.
S.TRANSL: When they washed themselves, the penis copied the others and made a dripping noise(?) into the water.

S.FORM: madudu ti Tjukutjuku; veseqitjen tua kasiw, sa sivuɫuq malipat i lauz.
S.PHON: madudu ti cukucuku; vəsəqicən tua kasiw, sa sivuʎuq maɭipat i ɭauz.
S.FORM: madudu ti Tjukutjuku; veseqitjen tua kasiw, sa sivuljuq malipat i lauz.
S.PHON: madudu ti ʦukuʦuku; vəsəqiʦən tua kasiw, sa sivuʎuq maɭipat i ɭauz.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku was angry and hit it with a piece of wood, and threw it out of sight downhill.

S.FORM: "adadadadadada," aya ti Qalitsi.
S.PHON: "adadadadadada," aja ti qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: "adadadadadada," aya ti Qalici.
S.PHON: "adadadadadada," aja ti qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: “Ow ow ow,” said Penis.

S.FORM: "ari saumaqi anga," aya.
S.PHON: "ari saumaqi aŋa," aja.
S.FORM: "ari saumaqi anga," aya.
S.PHON: "ari saumaqi aŋa," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go home,” they said.

S.FORM: ti Muakakai a sangasangasan, saka ti Tjukutjuku, saka ti Qalitsi, saka ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu a pangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: ti muakakai a saŋasaŋasan, saka ti cukucuku, saka ti qaɭiʦi, saka ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a paŋcəz i umaq.
S.FORM: ti Muakakai a sangasangasan, saka ti Tjukutjuku, saka ti Qalici, saka ti Kuljeljuljelju a pangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: ti muakakai a saŋasaŋasan, saka ti ʦukuʦuku, saka ti qaɭiʦi, saka ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a paŋʦəz i umaq.
S.TRANSL: Muakakai was first, then Tjukutjuku, then Penis, then Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu as they got home.

S.FORM: tjara maka tjumatjumaq anga azua vavayavayan katua tia Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu katia Qalitsi.
S.PHON: cara maka cumacumaq aŋa azua vavajavajan katua tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: tjara maka tjumatjumaq anga azua vavayavayan katua tia Kuljeljuljelju katia Qalici.
S.PHON: ʦara maka ʦumaʦumaq aŋa azua vavajavajan katua tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: They went round by their homes, the girls and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu and Penis.

S.FORM: mangtjez i tjumaq tia Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu katia Qalitsi.
S.PHON: maŋcəz i cumaq tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: mangtjez i tjumaq tia Kuljeljuljelju katia Qalici.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz i ʦumaq tia kuʎəʎuʎəʎu katia qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu and Penis got home.

S.FORM: "nakuya sun; masiaq itjen.
S.PHON: "nakuja sun; masiaq icən.
S.FORM: "nakuya sun; masiaq itjen.
S.PHON: "nakuja sun; masiaq iʦən.
S.TRANSL: “You’re bad. It was embarrassing for us.

S.FORM: su djungusen sakamaya tua tja sudju.
S.PHON: su ɉuŋusən sakamaja tua ca suɉu.
S.FORM: su djungusen sakamaya tua tja sudju.
S.PHON: su ɉuŋusən sakamaja tua ʦa suɉu.
S.TRANSL: You were getting close to(?) our girl-friends.

S.FORM: 'kaituvutuvungu,' nu ku su ayain, inika sun a kilangda."
S.PHON: 'kaituvutuvuŋu,' nu ku su ajain, inika sun a kiɭaŋda."
S.FORM: 'kaituvutuvungu,' nu ku su ayain, inika sun a kilangda."
S.PHON: ʔkaituvutuvuŋu,ʔ nu ku su ajain, inika sun a kiɭaŋda."
S.TRANSL: Although I said to you, ‘Stay in the container,’ you didn’t listen to me.”

S.FORM: "ari nu maya, saɫazuki," aya ti Qalitsi tjai Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: "ari nu maja, saʎazuki," aja ti qaɭiʦi cai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: "ari nu maya, saljazuki," aya ti Qalici tjai Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: "ari nu maja, saʎazuki," aja ti qaɭiʦi ʦai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: “Oh well, come on; let’s go to the plains,” said Penis to Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.

S.FORM: "ari nu maya," ayain; sa pangtjez i ɫazuk.
S.PHON: "ari nu maja," ajain; sa paŋcəz i ʎazuk.
S.FORM: "ari nu maya," ayain; sa pangtjez i ljazuk.
S.PHON: "ari nu maja," ajain; sa paŋʦəz i ʎazuk.
S.TRANSL: “All right, let’s go,” he replied. So they reached the plains.

S.FORM: sa "anema su katjenglayan, uɫa tjen a sema tsiam?"
S.PHON: sa "anəma su kacəŋɭajan, uʎa cən a səma ʦiam"
S.FORM: sa "anema su katjenglayan, ulja tjen a sema ciam?"
S.PHON: sa "anəma su kaʦəŋɭajan, uʎa ʦən a səma ʦiam"
S.TRANSL: “If we go to the shop, what do you fancy?”

S.FORM: "avan a tsiqaw katua ngiaw a ku katjenglayan."
S.PHON: "avan a ʦiqaw katua ŋiaw a ku kacəŋɭajan."
S.FORM: "avan a ciqaw katua ngiaw a ku katjenglayan."
S.PHON: "avan a ʦiqaw katua ŋiaw a ku kaʦəŋɭajan."
S.TRANSL: “I fancy a fish and a cat.”

S.FORM: saka amin nu velin.
S.PHON: saka amin nu vəɭin.
S.FORM: saka amin nu velin.
S.PHON: saka amin nu vəɭin.
S.TRANSL: So that’s all they bought.

S.FORM: sa vaik, sa pangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: sa vaik, sa paŋcəz i umaq.
S.FORM: sa vaik, sa pangtjez i umaq.
S.PHON: sa vaik, sa paŋʦəz i umaq.
S.TRANSL: They left and got back home.

S.FORM: "pasa puzayazayanu aken i tavi, sa pasazua-i uta azua ngiaw katua tsiqaw."
S.PHON: "pasa puzajazajanu akən i tavi, sa pasazua-i uta azua ŋiaw katua ʦiqaw."
S.FORM: "pasa puzayazayanu aken i tavi, sa pasazua-i uta azua ngiaw katua ciqaw."
S.PHON: "pasa puzajazajanu akən i tavi, sa pasazua-i uta azua ŋiaw katua ʦiqaw."
S.TRANSL: “Put me in the inner part of the house on the shelf, and put the cat and the fish there too.”

S.FORM: manu kikulat anan nua ngiaw, sa kasalisalitj a maka tua djui nua tsiqaw a qalitsi.
S.PHON: manu kikuɭat anan nua ŋiaw, sa kasaɭisaɭic a maka tua ɉui nua ʦiqaw a qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: manu kikulat anan nua ngiaw, sa kasalisalitj a maka tua djui nua ciqaw a qalici.
S.PHON: manu kikuɭat anan nua ŋiaw, sa kasaɭisaɭiʦ a maka tua ɉui nua ʦiqaw a qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: The cat kept frisking about(?), and the penis was tossed between the fins of the fish.

S.FORM: manu magutsal, manu metsautsau, saka ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a ngadan.
S.PHON: manu maguʦaɭ, manu məʦauʦau, saka ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a ŋadan.
S.FORM: manu magucal, manu mecaucau, saka ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a ngadan.
S.PHON: manu maguʦaɭ, manu məʦauʦau, saka ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a ŋadan.
S.TRANSL: And it burst open and turned into a person, whose name was Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: "keɫu anga, kaka.
S.PHON: "kəʎu aŋa, kaka.
S.FORM: "kelju anga, kaka.
S.PHON: "kəʎu aŋa, kaka.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, brother,

S.FORM: idan a ku situngan."
S.PHON: idan a ku situŋan."
S.FORM: idan a ku situngan."
S.PHON: idan a ku situŋan."
S.TRANSL: give me some clothes.”

S.FORM: manu mingtjus ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: manu miŋcus ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: manu mingtjus ti Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: manu miŋʦus ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu was startled.

S.FORM: "aku izuanga tsa ku saladj?" aya.
S.PHON: "aku izuaŋa ʦa ku saɭaɉ" aja.
S.FORM: "aku izuanga ca ku saladj?" aya.
S.PHON: "aku izuaŋa ʦa ku saɭaɉ" aja.
S.TRANSL: “Why have I now got this companion?” he said.

S.FORM: manu namagutsal anga azua qalitsi, sa kalevanga ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu sakamaya; sa paɫungpavi anga, sa pazangaqi anga.
S.PHON: manu namaguʦaɭ aŋa azua qaɭiʦi, sa kaɭəvaŋa ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu sakamaja; sa paʎuŋpavi aŋa, sa pazaŋaqi aŋa.
S.FORM: manu namagucal anga azua qalici, sa kalevanga ti Kuljeljuljelju sakamaya; sa paljungpavi anga, sa pazangaqi anga.
S.PHON: manu namaguʦaɭ aŋa azua qaɭiʦi, sa kaɭəvaŋa ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu sakamaja; sa paʎuŋpavi aŋa, sa pazaŋaqi aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Well the penis had burst open, and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu was very pleased. And he put on him a robe and a necklace.

S.FORM: "ari tsasavi anga sema sauɫauɫay a qemiladj a mapapaw ḍusa; uɫa ɫemengɫeng tia Muakakai kati Tjukutjuku."
S.PHON: "ari ʦasavi aŋa səma sauʎauʎaj a qəmiɭaɉ a mapapaw ɖusa; uʎa ʎəməŋʎəŋ tia muakakai kati cukucuku."
S.FORM: "ari casavi anga sema sauljauljay a qemiladj a mapapaw drusa; ulja ljemengljeng tia Muakakai kati Tjukutjuku."
S.PHON: "ari ʦasavi aŋa səma sauʎauʎaj a qəmiɭaɉ a mapapaw ɖusa; uʎa ʎəməŋʎəŋ tia muakakai kati ʦukuʦuku."
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go outside to the seats in the courtyard and sit there side by side; so that Muakakai and Tjukutjuku can see us.”

S.FORM: manu ɫengɫengen ni Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋən ni cukucuku.
S.FORM: manu ljengljengen ni Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋən ni ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: So Tjukutjuku saw them.

S.FORM: "tsasavu, kina," aya.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, kina," aja.
S.FORM: "casavu, kina," aya.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, kina," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Go outside, mother,” she said.

S.FORM: sa tsasaw a kina.
S.PHON: sa ʦasaw a kina.
S.FORM: sa casaw a kina.
S.PHON: sa ʦasaw a kina.
S.TRANSL: Mother went out.

S.FORM: "sa-u, kina, paɫiɫii azua i Vavuluvulungan ki tima zua qaɫaqaɫa."
S.PHON: "sa-u, kina, paʎiʎii azua i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan ki tima zua qaʎaqaʎa."
S.FORM: "sa-u, kina, paljiljii azua i Vavuluvulungan ki tima zua qaljaqalja."
S.PHON: "sa-u, kina, paʎiʎii azua i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan ki tima zua qaʎaqaʎa."
S.TRANSL: “Mother, go and see who the visitor is at the Vavuluvulungan house.”

S.FORM: manu sa sazua kina, sa pangtjez i Pakuvadavaday azua kina.
S.PHON: manu sa sazua kina, sa paŋcəz i pakuvadavadaj azua kina.
S.FORM: manu sa sazua kina, sa pangtjez i Pakuvadavaday azua kina.
S.PHON: manu sa sazua kina, sa paŋʦəz i pakuvadavadaj azua kina.
S.TRANSL: So mother went there, and she came back to the Pakuvadavaday house.

S.FORM: "qadjaw ki tima; ika ken a kemɫang."
S.PHON: "qaɉaw ki tima; ika kən a kəmʎaŋ."
S.FORM: "qadjaw ki tima; ika ken a kemljang."
S.PHON: "qaɉaw ki tima; ika kən a kəmʎaŋ."
S.TRANSL: “I don’t know who it is. I don’t know him.”

S.FORM: "sa-u; 'idu,' aya-u, kina."
S.PHON: "sa-u; 'idu,' aja-u, kina."
S.FORM: "sa-u; 'idu,' aya-u, kina."
S.PHON: "sa-u; ʔidu,ʔ aja-u, kina."
S.TRANSL: “Go and tell him to come here, mother.”

S.FORM: vaik a kina; djemaɫun i Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik a kina; ɉəmaʎun i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: vaik a kina; djemaljun i Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik a kina; ɉəmaʎun i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: Mother went, and reached the Vavuluvulungan house.

S.FORM: "ari, ɫa maḍusa, kivangavangi a sema tjanuamen," ayain.
S.PHON: "ari, ʎa maɖusa, kivaŋavaŋi a səma canuamən," ajain.
S.FORM: "ari, lja madrusa, kivangavangi a sema tjanuamen," ayain.
S.PHON: "ari, ʎa maɖusa, kivaŋavaŋi a səma ʦanuamən," ajain.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, you two, come for a visit to our house,” she said to them.

S.FORM: manu "uaa, masiaq amen.
S.PHON: manu "uaa, masiaq amən.
S.FORM: manu "uaa, masiaq amen.
S.PHON: manu "uaa, masiaq amən.
S.TRANSL: “Well, we’re ashamed to;

S.FORM: singidan tazua nu san tjautsiketsikel tjanuamen," aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: siŋidan tazua nu san cauʦikəʦikəɭ canuamən," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: singidan tazua nu san tjaucikecikel tjanuamen," aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: siŋidan tazua nu san ʦauʦikəʦikəɭ ʦanuamən," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: when was it that (or it’s not long since) you insulted(?) us?” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: sa vaik anga azua kina; mangtjez i Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa azua kina; maŋcəz i pakuvadavadaj.
S.FORM: sa vaik anga azua kina; mangtjez i Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa azua kina; maŋʦəz i pakuvadavadaj.
S.TRANSL: So mother left, and arrived at the Pakuvadavaday house.

S.FORM: "inika ui.
S.PHON: "inika ui.
S.FORM: "inika ui.
S.PHON: "inika ui.
S.TRANSL: “They don’t agree.

S.FORM: 'masiaq amen.
S.PHON: 'masiaq amən.
S.FORM: 'masiaq amen.
S.PHON: ʔmasiaq amən.
S.TRANSL: They say: ‘We’re ashamed;

S.FORM: ika magaɫu a nu san tjautsiketsikel,' aya, ɫa ku aɫak."
S.PHON: ika magaʎu a nu san cauʦikəʦikəɭ,' aja, ʎa ku aʎak."
S.FORM: ika magalju a nu san tjaucikecikel,' aya, lja ku aljak."
S.PHON: ika magaʎu a nu san ʦauʦikəʦikəɭ,ʔ aja, ʎa ku aʎak."
S.TRANSL: it wasn’t kind the way you insulted us,’ my child.”

S.FORM: "ki maya avan anga azua qalitsi makeɫang," aya maɫavar azua mareaɫaaɫak.
S.PHON: "ki maja avan aŋa azua qaɭiʦi makəʎaŋ," aja maʎavar azua marəaʎaaʎak.
S.FORM: "ki maya avan anga azua qalici makeljang," aya maljavar azua marealjaaljak.
S.PHON: "ki maja avan aŋa azua qaɭiʦi makəʎaŋ," aja maʎavar azua marəaʎaaʎak.
S.TRANSL: “I suppose it must be that penis,” said the mother and children to one another.

S.FORM: "sa-u, ɫiɫi-u, Lavuɫivuɫian, uɫa ken a puvavulungan."
S.PHON: "sa-u, ʎiʎi-u, ɭavuʎivuʎian, uʎa kən a puvavuɭuŋan."
S.FORM: "sa-u, ljilji-u, Lavuljivuljian, ulja ken a puvavulungan."
S.PHON: "sa-u, ʎiʎi-u, ɭavuʎivuʎian, uʎa kən a puvavuɭuŋan."
S.TRANSL: “Go and pay a visit, Lavuɫivuɫian (or go-between OA), so that I can marry without a betrothal present (or so that I can get into the Vavulungan household?).”

S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuɫian; mangtjez i Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian; maŋcəz i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuljian; mangtjez i Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian; maŋʦəz i vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: Lavuɫian went and reached the Vavuluvulungan house.

S.FORM: "aku a mangtjez sun, vuvu?" aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: "aku a maŋcəz sun, vuvu" aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: "aku a mangtjez sun, vuvu?" aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: "aku a maŋʦəz sun, vuvu" aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: “Why have you come, granny?” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: "'sa-u, ɫiɫi-u,' aya ti Tjukutjuku, 'uɫa ken a puvavulungan,' aya; avanu ku sisamaza."
S.PHON: "'sa-u, ʎiʎi-u,' aja ti cukucuku, 'uʎa kən a puvavuɭuŋan,' aja; avanu ku sisamaza."
S.FORM: "'sa-u, ljilji-u,' aya ti Tjukutjuku, 'ulja ken a puvavulungan,' aya; avanu ku sisamaza."
S.PHON: "ʔsa-u, ʎiʎi-u,ʔ aja ti ʦukuʦuku, ʔuʎa kən a puvavuɭuŋan,ʔ aja; avanu ku sisamaza."
S.TRANSL: “Tjukutjuku said: ‘Go and pay a visit, so that I can marry without a betrothal present.’ That’s why I’ve come.”

S.FORM: "ala iru ka ken a pukaui.
S.PHON: "aɭa iru ka kən a pukaui.
S.FORM: "ala iru ka ken a pukaui.
S.PHON: "aɭa iru ka kən a pukaui.
S.TRANSL: “But I certainly won’t agree.

S.FORM: ika magaɫu a nu tjautsiketsikel," aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: ika magaʎu a nu cauʦikəʦikəɭ," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: ika magalju a nu tjaucikecikel," aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: ika magaʎu a nu ʦauʦikəʦikəɭ," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: Your insults were not kind,” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: sa vaik (ti) sa Lavuɫian.
S.PHON: sa vaik (ti) sa ɭavuʎian.
S.FORM: sa vaik (ti) sa Lavuljian.
S.PHON: sa vaik (ti) sa ɭavuʎian.
S.TRANSL: So Lavuɫian left,

S.FORM: mangtjez i Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: maŋcəz i pakuvadavadaj.
S.FORM: mangtjez i Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz i pakuvadavadaj.
S.TRANSL: and reached the Pakuvadavaday house.

S.FORM: "ua, inika pukaui, ɫaɫak."
S.PHON: "ua, inika pukaui, ʎaʎak."
S.FORM: "ua, inika pukaui, ljaljak."
S.PHON: "ua, inika pukaui, ʎaʎak."
S.TRANSL: “Well, they don’t agree, child.”

S.FORM: "sa-u sakamaya, vuvu.
S.PHON: "sa-u sakamaja, vuvu.
S.FORM: "sa-u sakamaya, vuvu.
S.PHON: "sa-u sakamaja, vuvu.
S.TRANSL: “Go anyway, granny.

S.FORM: ku tjukuyavaw anga tua levut a ḍiḍi tu lima."
S.PHON: ku cukujavaw aŋa tua ɭəvut a ɖiɖi tu ɭima."
S.FORM: ku tjukuyavaw anga tua levut a dridri tu lima."
S.PHON: ku ʦukujavaw aŋa tua ɭəvut a ɖiɖi tu ɭima."
S.TRANSL: I’ll give five white pigs in compensation.”

S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuɫian a sema Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian a səma vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuljian a sema Vavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian a səma vavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: Lavuɫian went to the Vavuluvulungan house.

S.FORM: "'ku tjukuyavaw anga tua levut a ḍiḍi tu lima,' aya, ɫaɫak."
S.PHON: "'ku cukujavaw aŋa tua ɭəvut a ɖiɖi tu ɭima,' aja, ʎaʎak."
S.FORM: "'ku tjukuyavaw anga tua levut a dridri tu lima,' aya, ljaljak."
S.PHON: "ʔku ʦukujavaw aŋa tua ɭəvut a ɖiɖi tu ɭima,ʔ aja, ʎaʎak."
S.TRANSL: “‘I’ll give five white pigs as compensation,’ she says, child.”

S.FORM: "iru ka ken a pukaui angata," aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: "iru ka kən a pukaui aŋata," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: "iru ka ken a pukaui angata," aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: "iru ka kən a pukaui aŋata," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: “I most certainly won’t agree,” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuɫian a sema Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian a səma pakuvadavadaj.
S.FORM: vaik ti sa Lavuljian a sema Pakuvadavaday.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa ɭavuʎian a səma pakuvadavadaj.
S.TRANSL: Lavuɫian went back to the Pakuvadavaday house.

S.FORM: "inika pukaui a paraketj."
S.PHON: "inika pukaui a parakəc."
S.FORM: "inika pukaui a paraketj."
S.PHON: "inika pukaui a parakəʦ."
S.TRANSL: “He continues not to agree.”

S.FORM: "tjuari, ku sazuaw anga sakamaya," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "cuari, ku sazuaw aŋa sakamaja," aja ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: "tjuari, ku sazuaw anga sakamaya," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "ʦuari, ku sazuaw aŋa sakamaja," aja ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: “It can’t be helped(?); I’ll just go there myself,” said Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: kematsu tu qata tjanuita i kavatjes; saka avan a nanemanemanga sinu tjaɫavuluvulungan; sa vaik anga sema Vavuluvulungan ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: kəmaʦu tu qata canuita i kavacəs; saka avan a nanəmanəmaŋa sinu caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan; sa vaik aŋa səma vavuɭuvuɭuŋan ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: kemacu tu qata tjanuita i kavatjes; saka avan a nanemanemanga sinu tjaljavuluvulungan; sa vaik anga sema Vavuluvulungan ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: kəmaʦu tu qata ʦanuita i kavaʦəs; saka avan a nanəmanəmaŋa sinu ʦaʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan; sa vaik aŋa səma vavuɭuvuɭuŋan ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: She took a storage basket full of beads, and chose out all the oldest (most valuable) things. And so Tjukutjuku went to the Vavuluvulungan house.

S.FORM: qau sa pareputsekeɫ anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: qau sa parəpuʦəkəʎ aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: qau sa parepucekelj anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: qau sa parəpuʦəkəʎ aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: And in the end they were married.

S.FORM: saka izua, saka amin.
S.PHON: saka izua, saka amin.
S.FORM: saka izua, saka amin.
S.PHON: saka izua, saka amin.
S.TRANSL: And that’s the end.
